authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1926.20 - General safety and health provisions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the primary U.S. workplace-safety regulator. This construction rule requires contractor safety programs and frequent competent-person inspections that can block commercial construction progression.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Site safety prerequisite and stop-work handling
used_for: boundary between operations tracking and safety authority
refusal when unsafe conditions would change execution status

authoritative_sources.title: Obtaining a Permit
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official building-department guide shows that permit filing, plan review, and objection resolution belong to the applicant of record and AHJ, not this lane.
used_for: refusal of permit approval or code-interpretation requests

authoritative_sources.title: DOB NOW: Inspections Guide: Construction Inspection Sign Off Prior to Occupancy Request
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official DOB guide defines permit-entire status, Schedule A floor selection, objection handling, and construction-inspection signoff workflow prior to occupancy for commercial construction jobs.

authoritative_sources.title: Obtain a Certificate of Occupancy
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official occupancy guidance is authoritative for CO and TCO prerequisites, final signoffs, open-application and open-violation blockers, and occupancy boundaries that the lane must not overstate.
used_for: refusal of occupancy claims outside lane authority

authoritative_sources.title: Construction Process
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City School Construction Authority
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NYC SCA publishes detailed public-owner construction workflow guidance covering schedules, progress meetings, submittals, permits, inspections, change orders, safety plans, and closeout deliverables used in active commercial-style projects.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Execution planning, progress control, and closeout
used_for: closeout artifact expectations including warranties and training

authoritative_sources.title: GSA 184 - Construction Progress Report
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's official progress-report form and instructions define substantial completion posture, shop-drawing and material-delivery status, change-order effects, retainage, and adverse-progress documentation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Progress reporting and field-controls summary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Construction Quality Management for Contractors Student Study Guide
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USACE publishes an official contractor quality-management guide covering preparatory, initial, and follow-up control phases, daily quality-control reports, testing, and deficiency handling that directly constrain execution progression and rework.
used_for: daily QC reporting and test evidence

authoritative_sources.title: Construction Manual, Section 5-1: Project Records and Reports
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Caltrans publishes an official owner-side construction manual defining project records, daily reporting, status documentation, and change traceability used to support execution and field-controls discipline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Field record capture and exception traceability
used_for: daily report and project record expectations
record-of-truth expectations for rework and exception handling

workflow_stages.name: Execution intake confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the accepted commercial work package has the governing work order, area or floor, permit, trade, subcontractor, and jurisdiction context before progressing execution state.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Site-readiness and active-work validation
workflow_stages.description: Check daily reports, approved plans and submittals, permit-entire or permit status, utility and access conditions, safety observations, and inspection posture before allowing work to advance.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and rework handling
workflow_stages.description: Use field reports, deficiency evidence, change records, and QC or inspection results to decide whether work must hold, return for rework, or escalate.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Completion, turnover, and occupancy review
workflow_stages.description: Require final inspection, objection resolution, owner punch closure, O&M manuals, training acknowledgements, warranties, and occupancy posture before completion is recorded.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Authority escalation and completion reversal
workflow_stages.description: Stop or reverse progression when safety, inspection, permit, occupancy, trade, design, change-order, or policy conflicts materially change whether work can continue or close.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

artifacts: work orders, execution packages, and area-release records
permits, approved plans, approved submittals, and AHJ correspondence
inspection records, special inspection letters, objections, and deficiency logs
daily reports, site logs, QC reports, and safety observations
baseline schedules, look-aheads, subcontractor commitments, and progress-meeting notes
change orders, notices of direction, RFIs, and rework documentation
photos, test reports, commissioning records, and installation evidence
punch lists, O&M manuals, warranties, training acknowledgements, attic stock receipts, and turnover packages

allowed: Assess whether documented records support advancing, holding, reworking, or completing commercial construction execution work.
Prepare evidence-backed execution updates, completion records, exception notes, field-controls summaries, and escalation packages.
Request missing prerequisites and traceability records before changing lifecycle state.
escalate: Permit status, inspection requirements, site safety conditions, occupancy prerequisites, or closeout evidence materially change the answer.
Change-order, design, subcontractor, or policy conflicts affect whether work can advance or close.
Licensed trade, superintendent, permitting, inspection, safety, change-order, or closeout ownership is needed to resolve the issue.
refuse: Permit approval, code interpretation, occupancy approval, or regulator-facing commitments
Licensed trade signoff, engineer-of-record judgment, or design approval
Completion, closure, or acceptance without sufficient evidence

domain_failure_modes: Advancing work based on generic status labels instead of actual permit, site-readiness, inspection, and subcontractor records.
Marking work complete while objections, final inspection, owner punch, O&M manuals, warranties, or turnover evidence are still missing.
Failing to reopen or reverse lifecycle state when new safety observations, failed inspections, or occupancy objections change the answer.
Allowing unresolved change-order or scope-mismatch conditions to disappear from the execution queue.
Overreaching into code interpretation, licensed trade judgment, occupancy approval, design authority, or financial approval because the task sits in an operations queue.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies licensed trade or code-interpretation authority
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific permit closure, TCO timing, special-inspection sequencing, and occupancy restrictions vary materially across cities and project types.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline defines the need for permit, inspection, and occupancy evidence but does not eliminate local variation. Runtime must retrieve jurisdiction, project type, and tenant escalation policy before asserting project-specific occupancy posture.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific PMIS lifecycle names, owner punch conventions, and turnover-packet contents often exist outside public guidance.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: These controls are material to execution summaries and closeout readiness. The package therefore requires runtime retrieval of tenant naming, owner map, and completion templates rather than inferring them from public-owner examples.
